objective: Architectural sign-off on the ExploreConfig model + .grove/explore.json persistence (core::explore::config) for the S02 mcp-llm explorer subsystem.
key_changes: Architect approval granted — implementation is architecturally coherent, purely additive, and all gates independently verified.
ARCHITECT_APPROVAL.md written with rationale, deployment notes, and follow-ups.
findings: Purely additive core::explore module; lib.rs re-exports (ExploreConfig, Mode, Provider) match the crate's curated surface style — no existing surface touched.
.grove/explore.json sits beside .grove/grammars/, keeping all project-local grove state under one dotdir; right foundation for T02/T03.
Atomic save (temp .tmp.<pid> + rename) hardens over the registry's plain fs::write — candidate pattern to retrofit onto registry.rs later.
Field-named enum errors (RawExploreConfig manual Deserialize) and missing-file steering to `grove init --as mcp-llm` give clean fail-fast UX.
Dependency posture intact: no clap in grove-cst tree, no fastcontext in core/.
All gates green and independently re-verified: 8/8 explore tests, 146/146 workspace, clippy -D warnings clean.
Deployment: no version bump, no migrations, no user-facing change; explore.json created only on future save paths.
Follow-ups: Windows rename-over-existing semantics if that target matters; PID-only temp filename nonce; fix grove-core vs grove-cst naming nit in AC/PLAN templates.
